title: Facilitator Guide — Day 10 — Pitch · Empathy · Advise
prepChecklist: Confirm squads completed FEC Validation Lab and pitch draft in SPIKE Studio
Load faculty deck (14 slides) in Coach presentation mode
Review Squad XP leaderboard and mentor review panel
AV check for 5-minute squad pitch presentations
Confirm Empathy Lab task is live in participant Playbooks

sessionFlow.notes: Frame Discover ✓ Validate ✓ → Advise. Introduce Miguel and the 5-stage journey.

sessionFlow.activity: Market Validation Pitch — 5 min per squad + Q&A (slide 5 handoff)
sessionFlow.notes: SPIKE Studio presentation mode. Present evidence — customer quotes, FEC evolution, not ideas.
Empathy In Action → Think Like Miguel worksheet in SPIKE → squad discussion → reflect & share.
What would you ask Miguel? Preview Week 3 Financial Entrepreneurship — Opportunity Analysis Monday. Participants complete week wrap-up in Playbook.

sessionFlow.activity: Stage Gate II ceremony + Squad XP
sessionFlow.notes: Coach decision per squad: Not Ready / Almost Ready / Ready For Build. Mentor commendations.

debriefQuestions: Which squad showed the strongest interview evidence in their pitch?
What did squads learn about Miguel that they did not expect?
How does empathy change the way you would advise a client?
Who is ready to discover their business model in Week 3?
commonPitfalls: Skipping empathy workshop to extend pitch time
Pitching ideas instead of customer quotes and validated problems
Treating empathy map as a checklist instead of genuine perspective-taking
Individual grading instead of squad-first assessment
coachingTips: Use SPIKE presentation mode — deck aligns to pitch then workshop flow
After pitch block, return to slide 6 — do not skip the empathy lab
Stage gate is squad decision only — no individual scores
Empathy Lab saves automatically to portfolio
